articles.title: Prediction markets are breaking the news and becoming their own beat
articles.summary: Prediction markets are increasingly becoming the first place major news breaks, with traders moving odds before traditional media even catches wind of developing stories. This shift turns prediction platforms into must-watch news sources themselves, giving sharp bettors who monitor market movements a serious information edge over everyone else.

articles.title: Kalshi Doubles Down on Commodities, Announces Market Expansion and Dedicated Hub
articles.summary: Kalshi is launching a dedicated commodities trading hub and expanding its commodity market offerings. The move signals Kalshi's push to compete directly with traditional commodity exchanges and capture traders looking for regulated alternatives to offshore prediction markets.

articles.title: Kalshi Survivor 50 Episode 8 Predictions - Who Goes Home on Survivor Tonight?
articles.summary: Kalshi is running prediction markets on who gets voted off Survivor tonight in Episode 8 of Season 50. Reality TV betting markets are exploding as platforms discover that devoted fan bases will trade on literally anything they can obsess over.

articles.title: Fate of prediction markets in Nevada, elsewhere likely up to courts
articles.summary: Prediction markets face legal challenges in Nevada and other states, with courts set to determine whether these platforms can operate legally. The rulings will shape where Americans can legally bet on politics and other events, potentially opening new markets or forcing platforms to shut down operations.

articles.title: Prediction markets are a gamble we can't afford | Editorial
articles.summary: Some editorial board thinks prediction markets are too risky and we should avoid them entirely. Classic regulatory fear-mongering that could fuel more government crackdowns on platforms where people actually put money behind their predictions.

articles.title: Ohio slaps Kalshi with $5 million fine for unlicensed sports betting
articles.summary: Ohio regulators hit Kalshi with a $5 million penalty for allegedly operating unlicensed sports betting markets in the state. The fine signals growing regulatory scrutiny of prediction market platforms that blur the line between political forecasting and traditional gambling, potentially forcing clearer boundaries across the industry.

articles.title: Kalshi to Add More Commodity Markets as Global Turmoil Drives Retail Trading
articles.summary: Kalshi is expanding into commodity markets as retail traders pile into raw materials bets amid global economic uncertainty. The move signals prediction markets are evolving beyond politics into traditional finance territory, potentially attracting serious money from traders looking for regulated alternatives to crypto and meme stocks.

articles.title: Prediction Market Risk Is Hiding in Your Organization Whether You Know It or Not
articles.summary: Every company makes dozens of implicit predictions daily about customer behavior, market conditions, and operational outcomes, but most executives don't recognize these forecasts as bets that could blow up their strategy. Smart organizations are starting to formalize these hidden predictions into trackable markets, giving them better odds of spotting disasters before they hit the bottom line.

articles.title: Kalshi, Polymarket lobby as insider trading, betting eyed by Congress
articles.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket are racing to build political connections on Capitol Hill as lawmakers consider new rules for prediction markets. Whoever wins the lobbying game could shape regulations that determine which platforms thrive and what Americans can actually bet on.

articles.title: This Pennsylvania county is trying to stop prediction markets from influencing elections
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania county is attempting to ban or restrict prediction markets from operating during elections, claiming they manipulate voter behavior. If this gains traction, it could set a precedent for other jurisdictions to crack down on political betting markets just as they're gaining mainstream adoption.

articles.title: The casino-ification of literally everything : It's Been a Minute
articles.summary: Everything from stock trading apps to video games now uses casino-style mechanics like flashing lights, streaks, and instant gratification to keep users hooked and spending. This psychological playbook mirrors what makes prediction markets addictive, so understanding these triggers helps bettors recognize when they're being manipulated versus making rational decisions.

articles.title: Prediction markets will grow to $1 trillion by 2030, Bernstein estimates
articles.summary: Bernstein analysts predict the prediction markets industry will explode from its current size to $1 trillion by 2030. If they're right, we're looking at the mainstreaming of prediction markets from niche curiosity to legitimate financial infrastructure that could reshape how we price everything from elections to earnings.
